text:
Market today: Rotation day per Cramer's read - money moving within tech, not fleeing it. Cloud names under pressure (CoreWeave, Nebius hit by Meta threat), but cybersecurity and AI infrastructure holding better. Your chip-heavy book lives and dies on semis momentum, and that's mixed right now.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest alarm - leveraged 3X decay plus a -14.52% hole on margin means pain compounds fast; stop at $200 is your line. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already -13.72% with a razor-thin stop at $21.38 - one bad tick and you're out. SMH (-2.5%) is the quieter cousin of the same problem - semis softness dragging it. Margin amplifies all three.

Watch: $200 on SOXL and $21.38 on SPAL - both are stops you cannot ignore today. Also watch SMH for a bounce or break of $540.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers need stops respected NOW - protecting capital on margin beats hunting new trades.
plays: SOXL: stop at $200 is non-negotiable on margin - if it prints there, exit, no second-guessing. -14.5% in a 3X product decays hard.
SPAL: stop $21.38 is inches away right now - know your exit before the open, not after. New volatile position, margin context makes it urgent.
No new buys today - your leveraged positions need triage first; adding exposure here is the wrong order of operations.
